name: Deep-Dish Turkey Pot Pie
recipeInstructions:
To Make the Turkey Mixture:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
2. In a medium saucepan, combine 2 cups of the broth, celery, onions, and carrots. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat. Cover and simmer about 5 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
3. In a custard cup, stir the remaining 1/4 cup of broth and cornstarch until smooth. Slowly stir this into the broth-vegetable mixture.
4. Stir in the milk. Cook and stir over medium heat until the mixture comes to a boil.
5. Reduce heat. Cook and stir for 1 minute more.
6. Stir in turkey, peas, parsley, and sage. Transfer mixture to a shallow 2-quart casserole.
To Make the Pastry Crust:
7. Lay one sheet of phyllo dough on top of the turkey mixture.
8. Spray the dough with non-stick spray. Repeat layering and spraying two more times.
9. Fold or crumple the edges of the dough and tuck them inside the casserole dish.
10. Bake for 35-40 minutes, or until golden brown.
Note: I added potatoes, mixed vegetables, Tobasco sauce, and some liquid smoke to enhance flavor. Very good and light. Even though phyllo dough crust will get soft once refrigerated, it will get flaky again if reheated in the oven.
